sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The dark gray diamond at (35, 45) turned white again (id=34, color=W, pixels=8). So clicking (36, 45) re-activated it.
Now I see the pattern more clearly:
- The chain has multiple dark gray shapes that can be activated (turned white)
- Clicking one activates it and de-activates the previous one
- The activation moves down the chain
Current state:
- Dark gray diamond at (35, 45) is now WHITE (activated)
- Dark gray diamond at (51, 40) is still DARK GRAY (de-activated)
- Orange diamond at (28, 43) is still there
- Purple square at (32, 45) is still there
So the sequence for the purple chain seems to be:
1. Click purple square -> moves down, creates dark gray shapes
2. Click dark gray diamond at (36, 45) -> activates (white)
3. Click dark gray square at (41, 49) -> activates (white), previous goes back to dark gray
4. Click orange diamond outline at (51, 40) -> activates (white), previous goes back to dark gray
5. Click orange diamond at (28, 43) -> ??? (caused the white diamond to turn dark gray - this was wrong!)
6. Click dark gray diamond at (36, 45) -> re-activates (white)
So step 5 was a mistake. I shouldn't have clicked (28, 43). The chain was already complete after step 4.
But the level is not completed yet. So maybe there's another chain or another mechanic.
Looking at the image, I see:
- Purple ring at (15-21, 54-60) - this is still there
- Dark gray shape at (15-19, 41-48) with orange centers - this is still there
Maybe I need to click the purple ring? Or maybe I need to click the dark gray shape at the top?
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider the pattern. In the orange chain, after activating all the shapes, the level was completed. But here, the level is not completed after activating all the shapes in the purple chain.
Maybe the purple chain needs to be activated in a different order, or maybe there's a different target.
Looking at the image more carefully, I see the purple ring at (15-21, 54-60). This is similar to the purple ring in the orange chain. Maybe I need to click this?
Let me try clicking the center of the purple ring at (18, 57).
